tests.title: Reading Part 4
tests.description: You are going to read an article from which five sentences have been removed. Choose from the sentences A–H the one which fits each gap (16–20). There are three extra sentences which you do not need to use.

content.title: A Weekend Cycling Course
content.text: A Weekend Cycling Course was one of the most memorable experiences Sam had last year. Before the programme started, he felt nervous because he did not know anyone there. (16) [16]He thought the other participants might already be close friends and ignore him completely.[/16] Fortunately, the organisers introduced everyone carefully and encouraged people to work together. || At first, some activities were more difficult than Sam expected. He made several mistakes and sometimes needed extra help. (17) [17]Older participants explained each activity slowly and gave useful advice whenever he felt confused.[/17] After a few days, Sam became much more confident asking questions and trying new things. || Later in the week, the group completed a project together in small teams. Everyone shared ideas and helped solve problems during the activity. (18) [18]By the end of the programme, many participants had become close friends outside the activities too.[/18] The organisers were pleased to see people supporting each other so naturally. || When Sam returned home, he expected to forget most of what he had learned. Instead, he continued practising the new skills every weekend. (19) [19]He even showed some of the techniques to classmates who were interested in the programme.[/19] His parents were surprised by how enthusiastic he had become. || Now Sam recommends the programme to younger students at school. He says the experience helped him become more independent and confident. (20) [20]Meeting people with similar interests was the part he enjoyed most.[/20]

paragraphs.A: Some participants preferred spending time quietly on their own.
paragraphs.B: The weather became much colder later that evening.
paragraphs.C: The organisers cancelled one activity because of heavy rain.
paragraphs.D: He had never travelled outside his town before joining the programme.
paragraphs.E: Several experienced students offered support whenever he needed it.
paragraphs.F: The café near the activity centre closed earlier than expected.
paragraphs.G: Many students stayed in contact online after the programme finished.
paragraphs.H: Sam worried that nobody would want to speak to him.

questions.explanation: Sam feared people would ignore him.
It contrasts with Sam's growing confidence.
It contrasts with Sam enjoying shared interests.
